title: Over Bleeding During Periods: 10 Natural Herbal Tea Remedies
intro_desc: Herbal teas for over bleeding during periods are natural remedies that help alleviate excessive menstrual bleeding by regulating hormonal balance and soothing the uterine lining. The best herbal teas for this issue include Vitex agnus-castus, Cimicifuga racemosa, Trifolium pratense, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ginkgo biloba, Paeonia lactiflora, Angelica sinensis, Angelica archangelica, Althaea officinalis, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, over bleeding can lead to fatigue, weakness, and social isolation, negatively impacting daily life, work, and relationships.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus tea, also known as chasteberry tea, is a herbal remedy that has been used for centuries to help with menstrual problems, including heavy bleeding during periods. This tea is made from the fruit of the Vitex agnus-castus plant, which contains compounds that can help regulate hormonal imbalances in the body. One of the main reasons why this tea is effective for over bleeding is that it contains antioxidants and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the uterus. Vitex agnus-castus tea also has a natural ability to stimulate the production of progesterone, a hormone that helps to balance the levels of estrogen in the body. By balancing these hormone levels, the tea can help to reduce heavy bleeding and cramps during periods. Additionally, the tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the uterus, which can help to reduce bleeding and make periods more manageable. Some women also find that Vitex agnus-castus tea can help to regulate their menstrual cycle, leading to more regular and predictable periods.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids help with over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease heavy menstrual flow by promoting healthy blood clotting and tissue repair.
Iridoids help to reduce heavy bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to ease cramps and regulate blood flow.
Saponins are good for over bleeding during periods because they help to reduce inflammation in the uterus, which in turn helps to reduce heavy bleeding and promote a more balanced menstrual cycle.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal tea that helps with over bleeding during periods, start by drying the roots of the Althaea officinalis plant, also known as marshmallow root. You can do this by spreading the roots out in a single layer on a baking sheet and placing them in a low-temperature oven at 150-200 degrees Fahrenheit for 2-3 hours, or by air-drying them in a warm, dry place. Once the roots are completely dry, you can grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. To make the tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of the dried and ground marshmallow root in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, and drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and reduce excessive bleeding during your menstrual period. It's also important to note that Althaea officinalis tea is not a substitute for medical treatment, and you should consult with a healthcare professional before using it to manage heavy bleeding.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa tea is a herbal remedy that has been used for centuries to help with menstrual problems, including heavy bleeding. It contains a compound called actein, which has been shown to help reduce uterine contractions and improve blood flow to the uterus. This can help to slow down heavy bleeding and reduce discomfort during periods. The tea may also help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can help to ease cramps and other symptoms associated with heavy bleeding. Additionally, Cimicifuga racemosa tea has been found to have a balancing effect on hormone levels, which can help to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce the severity of heavy bleeding. Drinking Cimicifuga racemosa tea regularly may also help to reduce the risk of anemia, a condition that can occur when heavy bleeding leads to a lack of iron in the body.

Isoferulic acid is good for over bleeding during periods because it helps to reduce inflammation and ease uterine cramps, thus helping to regulate menstrual flow and prevent excessive bleeding.
Actein is good for over bleeding during periods because it helps to reduce inflammation and slow down blood flow, which can help to ease heavy and prolonged menstrual bleeding.
Tannins are good for over bleeding during periods because they help to reduce bleeding by shrinking blood vessels and also by acting as an astringent to stop the flow of blood.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal tea for period relief, start by gathering dried root of the marshmallow plant, also known as Althaea officinalis. This plant is rich in mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that can help soothe and calm the uterus during heavy periods. Begin by boiling 1 cup of water, then reduce the heat to a simmer and add 1-2 teaspoons of dried Althaea officinalis root. Let the root steep in the water for 5-7 minutes, allowing the mucilages to release into the liquid. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the root and any remaining particles. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce excessive bleeding and ease period symptoms. You can also combine Althaea officinalis with other herbs like chamomile or red clover to create a more soothing and nourishing tea blend.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis tea is a popular remedy for heavy menstrual bleeding. This tea is made from the roots of the Angelica plant, also known as Dong Quai. The plant contains compounds that help to strengthen the uterus and reduce blood loss. The main active ingredient, ferulic acid, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the uterus, which can cause heavy bleeding. The tea also contains other compounds like isoflavones and coumarins, which help to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce menstrual cramps. Drinking Angelica sinensis tea regularly may also help to improve blood flow to the uterus, which can help to reduce bleeding. Many women have reported that drinking this tea helps to reduce their menstrual flow and alleviate symptoms such as dizziness and fatigue that often accompany heavy periods. Additionally, the tea has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to help balance the body's energy and promote overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal tea for helping with over bleeding during periods, start by gathering clean and dry roots of the marshmallow plant, also known as Althaea officinalis. You can buy these roots from a herbal store or grow them in your garden. Rinse the roots with cold water, then chop them into small pieces to release their medicinal properties. In a medium-sized pot, combine 2 tablespoons of the chopped roots with 2 cups of water. Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. After the tea has cooled, strain it using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup. You can drink 1-2 cups of this tea a day to help reduce excessive bleeding during your period. It's essential to note that you should consult a healthcare professional before using this herbal tea as a treatment, especially if you have a history of heavy periods or are experiencing severe bleeding.

The anti-inflammatory properties of glycyrrhizin in licorice root tea can also help reduce menstrual cramps and ease other symptoms associated with heavy bleeding. This balance can lead to a reduction in bleeding and a decrease in the severity of menstrual cramps. Licorice root tea has also been used to help with PMS (premenstrual syndrome) symptoms, which can include heavy bleeding. Start by boiling one cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the roots' natural properties to infuse into the water. Next, strain the tea into a cup using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth to catch any loose root particles. You can enjoy the tea warm or let it cool to room temperature. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal tea that helps with heavy bleeding during periods, you'll need dried marshmallow root, which is the Latin name for Althaea officinalis. First, boil one cup of water in a kettle or on the stove. While the water is boiling, take one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root and add it to a tea infuser or a small muslin bag. Once the water has reached boiling point, remove it from the heat and pour it over the dried marshmallow root in the infuser or muslin bag. Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes, which allows the active ingredients to infuse into the water. After the steeping time is up, lift out the tea infuser or muslin bag and discard the dried root. Strain the tea into a cup to remove any remaining particles. Your Althaea officinalis herbal tea is now ready to drink. You can add a squeeze of lemon or honey to taste, but be sure to drink it while it's still warm as this can help the body to absorb the active ingredients more easily. Drinking this tea regularly can help to soothe and calm the uterus, which can help to reduce heavy bleeding during periods. However, please note that it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before trying any new herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking any medications.

supplementary_best: A combination of Vitex agnus-castus, Angelica sinensis, and Paeonia lactiflora can help with over bleeding during periods. Vitex agnus-castus is known to help balance hormones and regulate menstrual cycles. Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, is a traditional Chinese herb that helps to reduce menstrual bleeding and cramps. Paeonia lactiflora, or peony root, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help to ease heavy bleeding and cramps. This combination works well together because Vitex agnus-castus helps to regulate hormones, Angelica sinensis reduces bleeding, and Paeonia lactiflora eases cramps.
supplementary_avoid: If you have heavy bleeding during periods, you should avoid certain herbal teas that can increase bleeding or thin the blood. One such herb is Dong Quai (Polyscium cuspidatum), which is known to increase menstrual flow and may worsen heavy bleeding. Another herb to avoid is Red Clover (Trifolium pratense-free alternative - Trifolium micranthum), which contains isoflavones that can thin the blood and increase the risk of bleeding.  Also, avoid teas made from Meadowsweet (Filipendula ulmaria) and Willow Bark (Salix alba), as they contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin that can thin the blood and increase bleeding. Similarly, teas made from Ginger (Zingiber officinale) can increase stomach acidity, which may worsen heavy bleeding. Lastly, avoid teas made from Yarrow (Achillea millefolium), as it can increase menstrual flow and may worsen heavy bleeding.  It is essential to note that while these herbal teas may help with other health issues, they may not be suitable for individuals with heavy bleeding during periods. If you are experiencing heavy bleeding, consult with a healthcare professional before consuming any herbal teas. They can recommend suitable alternatives and provide guidance on safe usage.
supplementary_other_remedies: During periods, some women may experience over bleeding, which can be caused by hormonal imbalances, stress, or certain medical conditions. Besides drinking herbal teas, there are other natural remedies that can help alleviate this issue. One such remedy is applying heat to the lower abdomen, which can help relax the uterine muscles and increase blood flow to the area. This can be done using a heating pad or a warm compress wrapped in a towel. Another remedy is taking a warm bath or shower, which can help to relax the body and reduce stress, both of which can contribute to over bleeding. Additionally, practicing gentle exercises like yoga or meditation can also help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can in turn help to reduce bleeding. Some women also find relief by applying a paste made from turmeric, a spice that contains anti-inflammatory properties, to the lower abdomen. This paste can help to reduce inflammation and ease cramping, making it a helpful natural remedy for over bleeding.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience heavy bleeding during their periods may also suffer from other health issues that can be alleviated with medicinal herbs. One common problem is anemia, a condition where the body lacks enough red blood cells. The heavy blood loss can lead to iron deficiency, which is often treated with herbs like dandelion root and red clover. These herbs contain iron and other essential minerals that help increase red blood cell production.  Another issue is endometriosis, a condition where tissue from the uterus grows outside the uterus, leading to pain and heavy bleeding. Herbs like turmeric and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation.  Heavy bleeding can also lead to fatigue, which can be alleviated with herbs like ashwagandha and ginseng. These herbs help regulate energy levels and reduce stress.  Additionally, people with heavy bleeding may experience digestive issues like constipation or diarrhea, which can be treated with herbs like peppermint and licorice root. These herbs help soothe the digestive system and regulate bowel movements.  Finally, heavy bleeding can lead to hormonal imbalances, particularly with estrogen and progesterone. Herbs like vitex agnus-castus and wild yam help regulate hormone production and balance the menstrual cycle.
